what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment/section) from Tim in the dragnet-development channel, replying to item-79c533e9 with tentative ideas about block versioning, containment graphs, node identity, and addressing semantics.
observations: Authored by tim and posted in channel dragnet-development
Contains a commented_on link to board://item-79c533e9
Uses a [section] marker before the body
Written in an informal, stream-of-consciousness style with extensive hedging
Proposes versions as an orthogonal axis modeled by directed edges such as supersedes/succeeds/precedes
Describes a hierarchical containment graph where every block traces up to a root
Suggests comments attach to a specific node/version and replacements are derivable via typed edges and containment
Distinguishes a node from its position: the address is the node at that position
Uses a file-path analogy to argue that identity combines location, name, extension, and metadata
standouts: Versions as directed supersedes/succeeds/precedes edges between nodes rather than copies of one entity
Nearest-parent versioning with nested versions still addressable through containment
Address = node identity + position, decoupling the element from its location
Explicit request for pushback and alternative ideas due to author's stated 80% understanding
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history
Uses a [section] tag in the body, consistent with a section-style reply
Breaks conventional prose structure through long run-on sentences and mixed terminology
concerns: Terminology shifts between block, node, version, and address without precise definitions
No concrete examples, schema, or pseudocode to operationalize the proposed model
Heavy self-hedging may obscure which claims are intended as design assertions versus speculation
Relies on an external project reference (the vi visual project) that is not explained in this file
notes: Tim's self-hedged design note proposing that block changes become new nodes linked by directed version edges within a containment graph, while asking for pushback.
questions: What is the specific redundancy setup Tim is uncertain about?
What exactly is the 'vi visual project' and how does its block-versioning scheme map to this proposal?
How does 'nearest parent' version propagation work when multiple ancestors change?
What operations are allowed on a version chain and how is the active version selected?
How is node identity represented independently of position and display order?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to share tentative ideas and request pushback, and the content is an informal design brainstorm doing exactly that.
apparent_themes: Versioning as a graph of immutable nodes
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to advance a model where edits do not mutate blocks in place but instead create new, addressable nodes connected by typed succession edges, preserving comment anchoring and derivability through a containment graph.
novelty: It treats versions as an orthogonal directed-edge relationship rather than duplicate objects, and treats a file path as a compound identity made of address, name, extension, and metadata rather than a single locator.
contribution: The contribution is the design intuition that changes can be represented as new nodes linked by supersedes/succeeds/precedes edges, with comments anchored to specific nodes and replacement derivable through containment relationships.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development replying to item-79c533e9, proposing an immutable-node versioning model with directed supersedes/succeeds/precedes edges, hierarchical containment, and addresses that combine node identity with position.
intention_for_embedding: To share tentative ideas about block versioning, containment, and addressing semantics and to invite critique and alternatives.
